body.title: CLOSED: Route user turns cloud-first (D254) — verified, contract locked by tests
body.result: VERIFIED, NOT REFACTORED: routing at inference_gateway.py:651 already implements D254 correctly via 'not is_user_turn' guard. 3 tests added to lock in the contract (test_user_turn_cloud_first.py). The 20:17:27 incident was caused by upstream is_user_turn=False, not buggy routing — filed T-is-user-turn-flag-coverage for the actual gap. Commit 430d0ce2.
body.description:
**Approved 2026-05-03 (Akien — 'since he was replying to me, it should never have gone to local').** D254 specifies user turns route cloud-first. Current inference_gateway.py at line 650 fires local FIRST regardless of user_turn (the cloud branch at line 679 only runs if local already failed). That's a routing inversion that defeats D254.

Fix: when is_user_turn=True AND _cloud_ok AND self._t4, the cloud reasoner takes the FIRST attempt. Local is the fallback only when cloud is unavailable (no key, no _t4 instance, or cloud raised). The current local-first-then-cloud structure stays in place ONLY for non-user turns (background processing, reading, etc.).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/inference_gateway.py (the routing block around lines 640-740 — restructure the local-first / cloud-fallback / local-retry chain so user turns take cloud-first / local-fallback instead).
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/local-inference-no-timeouts (sibling rule — local takes whatever time it takes when it IS the right path, but user turns shouldn't take that path at all), D254 (user-turn cloud-first decision), theigors/rules/igor-constraints (Igor cognition path).
**Scope boundary:** IN: user-turn routing change to cloud-first; preserve non-user-turn routing as-is. OUT: timeout removal (separate ticket T-no-local-inference-timeouts). OUT: misreport fix (separate ticket T-inference-misreport-fix).
**Test plan:** unit test that with is_user_turn=True, the FIRST tier called is _t4 (cloud), not _t2 (local). Unit test that with is_user_turn=False (background), local is still called first. Integration: post a user query; confirm via reasoning_calls.log that the FIRST entry for that turn is cloud-source.
**Validation:** `reasoning_calls.log` shows source=cloud as the first entry for any user-turn invocation. **Cleanup:** none (test mocks). **Logging:** add `log.info` at each routing decision point that names which tier fires first and why (user_turn / quality / fallback / etc.) — observability for future routing audits.
